person.name: John Denton

memoryArtifacts.headline: John Brome p80 in Hawes genealogy

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
John Denton, Esq. was born circa 1451 at Foscott, Buckinghamshire, England.

«b»Marriage & Children«/b»
He married Isabel Brome, daughter of John Brome, Esq. and Beatrix Shirley, before 1472. 

They had 1 son & 2 daughters:
1.) Thomas

2.) Anne, wife of Sir Edward Greville

3.) Alice, wife of Nicholas Purefoy, Esq.

«b»Death«/b»
He left a will on 6 September 1493. His estate was probated on 17 November 1497.

nameMeanings.name: Denton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called. The vast majority, including those in Cambridgeshire, Cumbria, Dumfries, Durham, Kent, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Oxfordshire, Sussex, and Yorkshire are named from Old English denu ‘valley’ (see Dean 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. An isolated example in Northamptonshire appears in the Domesday Book as Dodintone meaning ‘enclosure, settlement associated with Dodda or Dudda’.

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John Denton was born in 1453, in Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Thomas Denton II, was 27 and his mother, Alison Dauncy, was 24. He married Isabell Brome before 1472, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1497, in Foscott, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 44.

ko.lifeSummary: John Denton(이)가 1453년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Denton II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alison Dauncy(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1472년 이전 영국에서 Isabell Brome(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1497년 영국 벅잉함실르 포스콧에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Denton родился в 1453 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Denton II было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alison Dauncy, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell Brome перед 1472 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1497 г. в местности фоскотт, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.
